Users._seedbalance_comment: Seed cash (USD) granted to a self-service registrant so they can trade the live market immediately. 0 = no seed fund. Tunable without a code change.

Db._percurrencygates_comment: Per-currency settlement-gate split (default off => identical concurrency, byte-identical). When true, each currency's per-(stock,currency) group settlement is throttled by an inner semaphore nested UNDER MaxConcurrentGroups, so a flood in one currency can't starve another's fill-rate. The global MaxConcurrentGroups still enforces the Npgsql-pool bound, so per-currency budgets may overlap. Override a currency's inner budget via PerCurrencyMaxGroups:<CCY> (e.g. USD/EUR); unset currencies use PerCurrencyMaxGroups:Default (75% of MaxConcurrentGroups).
Db._groupcommit_comment: Group-commit write-behind (default off => one root tx per (stock,currency) group, byte-identical). When Enabled, a currency's groups commit under ONE root tx (one fsync) per chunk of <= MaxBatch groups, each group a SAVEPOINT inside it so per-group rollback is preserved; cuts fsyncs/tick from groups.Count to ~#currencies. CRASH WINDOW: a process death before a chunk's root commit loses that currency's in-flight chunk, but the durable DB stays internally conserved (root tx is all-or-nothing) and the cache cold-loads from the DB on restart (cache == DB).

Bots._smoothedpricehalflife_comment: Realism/ret_acf: the EWMA price bots react to (anchors, recent-return). 0 = legacy fixed per-quote α=0.15 (tracks instantaneous within seconds → bots counter-trade their OWN 1-min impact → pins ret_acf_lag1≈-0.43). >0 = TIME-based half-life (sec): bots perceive a ~τ-lagged price regardless of quote rate, decoupling reaction from same-minute impact. A/B ≈60; baseline=0 byte-identical.
Bots._DecisionDistanceMult_comment: §P6 'tightness dial' over EVERY order-placement distance (limit tiers, stop/bracket-SL trigger, take-profits). ⚠ 0.2 IS THE VALIDATED PRODUCTION VALUE (2026-06-18 foundation config): it STACKS on the ×0.32 already baked into the per-bot Excel values (Tools/Config.py) for an effective ×0.064 — limit orders ~5× closer = the ret_acf/liveliness breakthrough. Do NOT 'restore' this to 1.0 (that would 5×-widen every placement distance and undo the foundation). Fold-into-Excel + dial→1.0 is queued for the next reseed (multiplier→excel cleanup).
Bots._marketprobmult_comment: Realism: global multiplier on each bot's UseMarketProb (more market/taker orders ⇒ more volume + fewer flat candles). 1.0 = unchanged.
Bots._cashinjection_comment: Nominal-growth cash top-ups. IntervalMinutes = cycle cadence (was a hard-coded 1h const; lowered to 30m for more buying/volume). Per-bot frequency/amount gate each deposit within a cycle (seeded in Tools).

Liquidity._comment: Limit-ladder-only multipliers over per-bot Excel values. OffsetMult rests limit orders further/closer (wider/narrower band) — now a fine-tune ON TOP of the global Bots:DecisionDistanceMult dial, so keep it 1.0 unless tuning limits independently. MaxOpenMult allows more rungs. MaxSweepFractionOfDepth caps a single bot MARKET order to that fraction of resting opposite-side depth (structural anti-sweep).

Tiers._comment: §P6 tiered limit ladder: per-order probability of resting in the Close vs Mid band (Far = remainder). Per-tier %-bands are PER-BOT (AIUser.*LimitMin/MaxPrc); Close reuses Min/MaxLimitOffsetPrc.

Fundamental._comment: §P6 slowly-drifting per-stock fundamental the value anchor tracks. OU walk reverting to seed, hard-clamped to seed*[1±Band]. Theta = reversion pull/step, Sigma = shock as fraction of seed/step (scaled per-stock by personality). Enabled=false reverts to the fixed seed (pre-P6).

Personality._comment: §P6 per-stock volatility class (Calm..Meme) scaling sentiment amplitude, fundamental drift, and the value-band veto. Deterministic from stock id. Enabled=false makes every stock neutral.

StopBreaker._comment: §P6 stop-promotion circuit breaker: hard cap on stop promotions per (stock,currency) per window. Excess fires are deferred and re-fed next window (never dropped) so a cascade can't fire dozens at once. Only bites during a cascade.

Bots._geometricBandComment: §geometric price-bands: true ⇒ the ValueAnchor caps (OverheatCap, AbsoluteCapMax) + the adaptive runaway guard are LOG-SYMMETRIC — a cap C means ×(1+C) up / ÷(1+C) down (so AbsoluteCapMax=2.0 ⇒ ×3 up / ÷3 down), not linear ±C (whose −C floor goes negative past 100%). Default false ⇒ byte-identical linear behaviour.
Bots._dipBuyComment: §dip-buy (down-drift fix): when >0, a bot BELOW its value-anchor holding cash above its MaxCashReserve boosts buy-probability by DipBuyStrength × dip-depth × excess-cash-fraction — deploying the hoarded/injected idle cash as DEMAND on dips (the two-sided support the net-long, cash-hoarding fleet lacks). Neutralises the systematic down-drift WITHOUT the anchor's move-killing spring. Dip-gated (no up-side pressure) + self-limiting (spends the cash). 0 = off (byte-identical).
Bots._bearShortComment: §bear-short (up/down symmetry fix): when >0, a bot's advanced-order roll gets an extra SHORT bucket sized BearShortStrength × watchlist-bearishness, so a FLAT bearish bot opens a cash-collateralized short instead of no-op'ing (it holds no inventory to sell). Gives the sell side the same 'always able to act' firepower the cash-abundant buy side has ⇒ down-moves become as sharp as up-moves (per-move up was ~2.5× down). Rides the normal match/settle path (CK-safe); the ÷3 geometric-floor veto bounds the short so it can't runaway. 0 = off (byte-identical).
Bots._trendFollowerComment: §trend-follower (realism overhaul step 3): Enabled=true adds a hash-selected chartist cohort (CohortFraction of bots) that CHASES recent price momentum (directional += Strength * momentumSignal[±5% move → ±1]), inducing the positive short-term autocorrelation that nets the value-investors' bounce toward realistic ~0 (the fix for the over-negative 1-min ret_acf). ContrarianFraction of the cohort FADES instead + a per-bot strength stagger (0.5-1.5x) keeps the herd from moving in lockstep (a synchronized 'marching band' reads as fake). POSITIVE FEEDBACK - bounded by momentumSignal's +-1 clamp + the x3 elastic band; tune Strength UP a geometric ladder and STOP one rung below any ret_acf-crosses-0 / cap-touch. Enabled=false or Strength/CohortFraction 0 = byte-identical.

TrendFollower._sharedChaseComment: §shared-chase (step 4, cross-stock correlation): the SAME taker-flow idea on the SHARED global signal (BotSentimentService.GlobalSignal() = global OU ring sum + global shock, identical for every stock this tick, ~+-0.2-0.4). SharedChaseWeight>0 makes a cohort member, when |global| >= TakerThreshold AND |global| >= |momentum| (prefer the stronger signal), with prob=clamp(SharedChaseWeight*|global|,0,1) OVERRIDE to a slippage-market taker in the GLOBAL direction (contrarian fades). Because the signal is SHARED, the whole cohort across ALL stocks aggresses the same way at once = fleet-wide correlated taker flow - the channel shared sentiment (a buyProb tilt) is absorbed by the book but shared FLOW is not. Weight needs to be larger (~1.5-3) since |global| < |momentum|. 0 = no override, no extra draw = byte-identical. Pair with Bots:Sentiment:GlobalShock / a raised global sigma for a strong shared driver.
TrendFollower._takerCouplingComment: §taker-coupling (step 3 v2): the buyProb tilt above is ABSORBED (a chartist's extra buying rests as a LIMIT order). Price impact = direction x size x taker-ness, so TakerCoupling=true makes a member CROSS THE SPREAD ('conviction crosses the spread', Chiarella-Iori): when |momentumSignal| >= TakerThreshold, with prob = clamp(Strength*|momentumSignal|,0,1) the order is OVERRIDDEN to a slippage-market taker in the momentum direction (a contrarian member fades). Only takers move price. Phase 1 = type+direction (NOT size). Off = no override, no extra draw = byte-identical.

ValueAnchor._comment: Restoring force toward each stock's seed/fundamental price. Strength = max buy/sell-prob tilt; Scale = deviation fraction at which it saturates. TargetSelection concentrates the anchor via stock selection (destabilizing at high gain — keep off). 0 = off (driftless walk). OverheatCap is the per-stock veto threshold (deviation from anchor); AbsoluteCapMax (0 = off) hard-caps the EFFECTIVE post-personality cap so OverheatCapMult on Volatile/Meme can't widen the band past the absolute promise. UsePreviousDayAverage switches the long-anchor TARGET from the OU walk to a per-stock daily TWAP (hard-clamped to seed × [1±MaxDailyDrift]) maintained by BotPriceMemoryService — rotates every DayLengthHours under the DayBoundaryMode policy (ServiceStart = reproducible / soak-friendly; UtcMidnight = realistic / production). Defaults preserve today's behaviour (OU walk + no daily rotation).
ValueAnchor._elastic_comment: Elastic-band anchor (realism overhaul step 2): Elastic=true replaces the LINEAR tilt with a nonlinear soft-wall — zero restoring pull within +-ElasticDeadbandPrc of fundamental (small intraday + multi-day tens-of-% moves wander free, no per-tick snap-back = kills the -0.43 1-min bounce), then tilt = Strength*sign*(excess/Scale)^ElasticPower beyond the band (ElasticPower>1 = gentle just past the band, stiff far out = the elastic band + a sell-driver on big up-runs / buy-driver on big down-runs, bounding the multi-day/28-day range well inside the AbsoluteCapMax x3). Default Elastic=false = today's linear path, byte-identical. TUNE Deadband/Power/Scale jointly (Scale interacts strongly with how fast it stiffens).

Adaptive._comment: Adaptive (path-dependent) anchor: the overheat cap re-centers on a fast EWMA of the traded price (FastHalfLifeSec) so genuine moves re-rate the level and STICK (bigger bodies, smaller wicks) instead of snapping back to seed. BlendWeight (0..1) = how much it re-rates (0 = today's fixed-seed cap). MaxTotalExcursion hard-bounds total displacement from the ORIGINAL seed (independent runaway veto) so the market can't walk away. Default OFF ⇒ byte-identical. Co-runs with CapFromSeed=true / RecentAnchor.

RecentAnchor._comment: Medium-term mean-reversion to the per-stock recent EWMA price. The missing negative-feedback against fast moves — a stock that ran +30% in 5 min faces a strong pull back toward its 30-min EWMA so trends fade away from the OverheatCap instead of pinning at it. Strength = max prob tilt; Scale = deviation fraction at which it saturates (separate from ValueAnchor:Scale). Default OFF (BotPriceMemoryService.Tick is observationally inert when this AND ValueAnchor:UsePreviousDayAverage are both off).

DirectionalPressure._comment: Hybrid pressure formula: when on, directional+herd push the cohort multiplicatively-around-0.5 (sell-side spread = buy-side spread by construction) so the sell-biased half of the cohort preserves its counter-pressure under strong buy directional — the natural damping the additive form crushes by saturation. Anchors stay additive (structural override of personality). DiversityGain is the amplitude (1.0 = baseline scaling, higher = larger cohort spread at extremes). Default OFF ⇒ byte-identical to today's additive formula.

Advanced._comment: Per-kind probabilities are PER-BOT now (AIUser.*Prob, seeded by strategy in Tools/Person.py). StopOffset + TpOffset here are now only FALLBACKS for un-regenerated bots — the real per-bot distances are baked into the Excel pipeline (StopDistance*/TpOffset* columns). Kept tightened to match.
Advanced._batchArmsComment: A1a: route StopMarketSell/TrailingStopSell arms through the batched engine path (share pre-reserve + one bulk-insert tx). Default false until the PROD soak gate passes; flip off to revert to per-order arms instantly.
Advanced._bracketBatchComment: A1b/A1c: route LongBracket/ShortBracket placements through the batched engine path (PlaceBracketBatchAsync amortises the child-leg insert into one bulk tx). Default false until the conservation soak gate passes; flip off to revert to per-order bracket placement instantly. NOTE: flat market-short opens are NO LONGER routed here — they have their own BatchShortOpens flag below (Slice 2).
Advanced._batchBuyStopsComment: A1b (Phase 2): route StopMarketBuy arms through the batched engine path (ArmStopBuyBatchAsync: FUND pre-reserve under the per-user fund gate + one bulk-insert tx). Default false until its conservation soak gate passes; flip off to revert to per-order buy-stops. NOTE Phase 1 is the bigger win with NO new code: flip BracketBatch above to true after its own conservation soak.
Advanced._batchShortOpensComment: Slice 2: route flat market-short opens through the batched engine path (PlaceMarketShortBatchAsync: no placement reserve — short collateral is reserved at FILL inside one group tx per (stockId,currency), amortised to one root commit/fsync per currency shard under Db:GroupCommit:Enabled). Decoupled from BracketBatch so the adv-phase short lever is independently measurable. Default false until the conservation soak gate passes (short-heavy fleet: lower BuyStopFraction; watch CK_Positions/CK_Funds + the reservation reconcile WARN + the F1 same-user-buyer+seller probe); flip off to revert to per-order short opens instantly.
Advanced._buyStopFractionComment: Taker-flow symmetry (council design): fraction [0..1] of bot protective-TRIGGER decisions routed to a BUY-stop (up-trigger, capped market buy above market, cash-gated: covers a short if held else a breakout buy) vs a sell-stop (down-trigger, share-gated). A single seeded draw picks the direction, then the matching resource is required with NO fallback, so the realized buy/sell split is this fraction (not the ~98%-long inventory). Fixes the down-drift from sell-only protective stops (80k sell-stops / 0 buy-stops). 0 => legacy sell-only, byte-identical. Start conservative (~0.35): the buy side is structurally stronger (cash ~unlimited + momentum), so taker-VOLUME balance is the soak gate. Watch for up-runaway; slippage cap + StopBreaker bound cascades.
Advanced._inventoryBiasShortMultComment: Round 3 Q1: short-side threshold divisor for the InventoryBias asymmetry. 7-run post-round-2 characterisation found 2.0 as the sweet spot — bear-tail equivalent to baseline, CK clean over 164k trades. Setting kept as a knob so a future tuning can roll back to 1.0 (round-2 baseline) or extend to 2.5 (marginal further benefit but CK trigger seen). 4.0 was counterproductive.

Arbitrage._comment: §3.7 cross-listing arbitrage cohort. Enabled gates the whole feature. ValueDrainCeilingPct is the value-drain guard: when the cohort+house wealth exceeds this % of total market value the throttle engages and the cohort stops opening new round-trips. ConversionSkewBand is how lopsided a bot's USD/EUR cash mix must get before it rebalances through the FX desk (which funds the house). NOTE: lowered from Ultraplan's 0.15 — the per-bot cash skew oscillates only ~0.50-0.53, so a 0.15 band (trigger 0.575) effectively never fires and the house never accrues; 0.03 (trigger 0.515) rebalances regularly so the house earns the spread and bots stay near 50/50.
Arbitrage._batchLegsComment: STRETCH (unbaked): run the arb cohort's round-trip entries as two batched passes — all eligible bots' leg1 buys in one PlaceAndMatchBatch, then leg2 sells sized per-bot from each leg1 fill in a second batched pass (preserves the fill dependency). Flatten + FX rebalance stay per-bot. Default false; only flip on for a measurement soak — NOT bake-gated this round.
Arbitrage._sharedScanComment: Phase 2a (unbaked): compute the cross-listed opp/gap map ONCE per tick and share it across the arb cohort — today CollectOppsAsync re-scans all 20 books per-bot (5x redundant). Byte-identical via intra-tick self-invalidation after each bot's legs; proven by SharedScanEquivalenceTests. Default false; flip on only for a measurement soak.
Arbitrage._eventDrivenComment: Phase 2b (optional, requires SharedScan): skip the per-tick entry scan on quiet ticks via a subscription-free OrderBook.BookVersion sample + FX-dirty flag. Default false; only pursue if the post-2a re-measure shows residual scan cost.
Arbitrage._batchRebalanceComment: Phase 1 (optional, last): settle the cohort's per-tick FX-rebalance ConvertAsync calls in one root tx with deferred post-commit side-effects. Near-zero win vs the scan-dominated tick + money-path risk; default false.

MarketMaker._comment: §mm-cohort: the all-weather two-sided resting-liquidity cohort (AiStrategy.MarketMakerHouse=6), seeded SEPARATELY from the random fleet like Arbitrage. Enabled is the master runtime gate (the A/B toggle); default OFF and — with no strategy-6 bots seeded — byte-identical. Quotes around a reference that survives a one-sided book (mid → last-trade → EWMA → seed) so it supplies asks into an up-shock. HalfSpreadBps = half the target touch spread; QuoteSize = shares per side before skew/caps; SkewBps = inventory price-skew toward flat; RequoteThresholdBps = reprice-on-move hysteresis; MaxCashFrac caps bid + short-collateral notional vs available cash; PriceJitterBps disperses quotes off the round grid (RNG-free); OneSidedWidenMult widens the spread when the reference came from a one-sided/cold rung; UseMicro uses the size-weighted micro-price for the two-sided rung; Probe enables the soak telemetry line.

BankEstimate._comment: §bank-estimate: a 'dominant house analyst' that periodically republishes a per-stock fair-value ESTIMATE (a fractional deviation from seed). The slow OU value-anchor (FundamentalService) is pivoted to track it, and the Rotator cohort trades INTO the price-vs-estimate gap — the value + momentum legs of one coupled mechanism. Enabled is the master gate; default OFF ⇒ the anchor target stays the seed ⇒ byte-identical. Alpha weights the (ZERO-MEANED) per-stock sentiment vs the prior estimate; PoissonMeanIntervalSec is the IRREGULAR republish cadence (~20-40s, not clockwork); WrongnessFraction is the idiosyncratic variance so the estimate is sometimes WRONG (the GAP is the tradeable feature); SectorCount re-uses the exog sector grouping (stockId % N) so a whole sector re-rates together (default = the ExogShock SectorCount).
BankEstimate._comment_seedall: §soak: SeedAllOnStart publishes an estimate for EVERY stock on the first tick (bypass the ~10min Poisson dribble) so short A/B soaks aren't signal-starved to the few arrived stocks. Prod = false (warms over its long run); enable via env for soaks.
BankEstimate._comment_sectordrift: §P4 the per-sector shared-drift walk = the SHARED component of the gap signal the Conviction cohort chases. SectorDriftCap bounds the walk (historical 0.03 = +-3% sector re-rating); SectorStepScale multiplies the per-tick step +-(WrongnessFraction*0.01) (historical 1.0). Defaults = byte-identical. Raise both for STRONGER/more persistent sector re-ratings -> more sector-coherent Conviction taker flow -> higher intra-sector correlation (tune via soak; too high = sectors visibly detach from fundamentals).
BankEstimate._comment_sectorevent: §tails: RARE heavy-tailed sector re-rating EVENTS (mixture step: mostly the normal walk, occasionally |step|*SectorEventMult with P(down)=SectorEventDownBias). Rides the sector->estimate->conviction-taker transmission whose SUSTAINED flow survives the book-refill wall that absorbed one-shot jumps => fat tails + elevator-down + sector-wide correlation spikes in one lever. EventProb=0 (default) draws NO extra RNG = byte-identical. Events saturate at SectorDriftCap (raise it if events feel clipped).

Bots._bankestimate_cadence_note: 2026-07-09 council 5/5 (4/5 rejected the 6h instinct): 30s republish read as noise, not fundamentals. Set PoissonMeanIntervalSec 30->600 (10min) + Alpha 0.3->0.35 + WrongnessFraction 0.15->0.12 so the slower cadence stays meaningful without freezing the estimate (effective smoothing half-life = cadence x Alpha ~= 20-30min = reads slow, rotators stay live, ~24min warm-up). 6h was rejected: the rotator gap closes in minutes then the cohort goes dormant for 6h + a multi-hour restart dead-zone. FOLLOW-UP (Kiesh-gated): SectorCount 1->6 activates the dormant sector co-rerating = the actual cross-stock correlation story (currently dead at SectorCount=1). Rollback = 30/0.3/0.15 + restart.

Rotator._comment: §rotator (redesign, council 5/5): estimate-driven cohort (AiStrategy.Rotator=7), seeded separately. Enabled = master gate (default OFF, byte-identical with no strategy-7 bots). Each firing bot picks ONE favoured name to BUY + ONE disfavoured to SELL, rank-weighted, via batched MARKET orders (sell-then-buy, CK-safe). TurnoverFraction bounds each order to that fraction of the seed notional (buy) / held qty (sell) — the fix for the all-in cash-bomb that froze the loop + ran the P&L away; sweep 0.05-0.20. ParticipationFraction = the flow/correlation valve, SCALER-COUPLED: effectivePF = max(ParticipationFloor, PF*(1-load)) so it self-throttles under load, floored so flow never dies. SeedBalance* = the turnover notional base (match Tools/Config.py ROTATOR_SEED_BALANCE_*). Only meaningful with Bots:BankEstimate:Enabled on.
Rotator._useloadewma_comment: §B-3: read the scaler's smoothed LoadFractionEwma instead of the raw up-to-2s-stale LastLoadFraction for the scaler-coupled participation valve, damping the phase-lag hunting from two controllers on one stale signal. Default false => byte-identical; the participationFloor interlock is unchanged either way.

Conviction._comment: §conviction: a SEPARATE cohort (AiStrategy.Conviction=8) of realistic cash-heavy discretionary traders that rotate into 'good plays' OCCASIONALLY on sentiment + sector-momentum with per-bot personality and REAL directional risk (win-rate << 100%) — NOT the mechanical Rotator. Enabled = master gate (default OFF, byte-identical with no strategy-8 bots). Every acted order is an AGGRESSIVE TAKER (true-market) order so the value-anchor can't absorb it. Hot = Wsec*sectorSentiment + Wmom*slowSlope + Wglobal*globalSignal + Widio*idio - Wover*max(0,-gap): sentiment/sector-momentum LED (a fader NEGATES Wsec/Wmom); the -Wover gap term is a one-way VETO against chasing a name already above the bank estimate (never pushes a buy) — keep Wsec+Wmom >> Wover. Per-bot dials are HASHED from the aiUserId (no seed columns): CashFloorPct in [Base,Base+0.30], RiskAppetite in [Base,Base+0.20] hard-capped 0.25, ConvictionBar in [Base,Base+0.09], SentimentSens [0.3,1.5], CheckInMeanSec in [Base,Base+9600] (20min-3h), Lean chaser65/fader35. ExitBar/StopOvervaluation drive the memoryless sell. SeedBalance* = the per-bot bet notional base (match Tools/Config.py CONVICTION_SEED_BALANCE_*). CK-safe: sell-then-buy batched, buys from fresh cash minus the floor. Only meaningful with Bots:BankEstimate:Enabled on (the gap guardrail).
Conviction._comment_p1: §P1 hold-horizon (default off = original memoryless exit). ON: hold THROUGH DRAWDOWNS — the soft thesis-decay exit waits out a per-bot hashed HoldSec in [HoldMinSec, HoldMaxSec] since entry (Position.UpdatedAt); hard overvaluation exits bypass it. Produces real losses (win-rate << 100%) + longer holds that rotate.
Conviction._comment_p2: §P2 conviction-scaled sizing (default off = flat RiskAppetite sizing). ON: bet = MaxDeploy·z^SizingGamma of the cash headroom, z=clamp(strength/ConvScale,0,1), strength = Hot above the effective bar ⇒ most plays tiny, rare exceptional convictions approach MaxDeploy. Cash floor still applies (removed in cash-to-zero phase).
Conviction._comment_p3: §P3 shorting ROUTE (default off = long-only, byte-identical). ON: a fire may also SHORT the single most-OVERVALUED name it is FLAT in (overvaluation >= ShortBar, momentum not rising) via the batched market-short route, and COVER a held short once overvaluation reverts below 0.5*ShortBar or momentum turns up. Shorts are sized small = ShortRiskFraction*RiskAppetite*seed so the cover buyback is affordable. CK-safe: sell/cover then buy/short; collateral reserved at fill, released by the settler on cover. This phase proves the short route (CK, collateral invariant, F1 rollback rate, perf) — the full signed two-way Hot is a later phase.
Conviction._comment_p4: §P4 SIGNED two-way Hot + conviction-led probabilistic lifecycle (default off = the v1/P1-P3 path byte-identical). ON it SUPERSEDES the P1 hold-gate, the P3 standalone short trigger and the deterministic exit scan: HotSigned = Wgap*ln(est/price) + Wsec*sectorSent + Wglobal*global + Wmom*leanF*mom + Wown*leanF*ownSent + Wnoise*freshNoise (shared carriers NOT lean-flipped = correlation; log gap = long/short symmetric; noise = entry-side mistakes only). Sign chooses long/short (short bar = long bar * ShortBarMult, flat-only), magnitude drives P2 sizing. TWO CLOCKS on one pass: entries stay on CheckInMeanSec, held positions are reviewed every ~ReviewMeanSec (cheap entry-record walk). EXIT = additive competing hazards per review: ExitBaseHazard*(heldSec/HoldSec)^ExitTimeExp (soft time-stop centered on the per-bot HoldSec) + ExitFlipGain*conviction-turned-against (the strong trigger) + ExitSatisfyGain when the ENTRY gap closed (|gap|<=SatisfiedBand, entry-record-aware — no born-satisfied churn). Guardrails: close prob is load-scaled, MinHoldSec floor, MaxExitFractionPerPass cohort cap (hard gap-past-stop exits bypass all three). Entry records anchor heldSec (NOT Position.UpdatedAt).
Conviction._comment_p5: §P5 basket entries (default 1 = the single-best legacy pick, byte-identical). K>1: a fire opens a small BASKET of the top-K names above the bar; the per-fire deployment is SPLIT across the basket (budget/basketSize) so a basket is MANY SMALLER plays, not K x the risk.

StrategyTelemetry._comment: §per-strategy telemetry: emit a BotStratPerf line alongside BotEconomy each snapshot — portfolio-Δ vs seed baseline, bot count, win-rate and session trades per AiStrategy — so we can validate the Rotator + the MM cohort and check no strategy strip-mines the rest. O(bots), in-memory, no extra DB I/O. Default ON.

Bots._v2_comment: §bot-market-realism-v2: emergent-correlation pillars that break the LLN flat chart. VALIDATED ON (2026-06-10 2.5h soak: steady-state candle shape == random walk, conservation clean, drift within budget). Each flag still reproduces today's behaviour byte-identically when OFF (rollback path). Inertia (A1) + Herding (A2) make the chart MOVE; Activity (B) makes volume breathe; Range (C) prints wicks. SentimentMaxBias lowered to 0.10 so the regime augments rather than double-counts the directional weight.

Imbalance._comment: Pillar A — makes the chart MOVE via genuine order-flow imbalance (persistence + herding), not by tuning per-bot probabilities. Inertia (A1) is the MVP. Herding (A2) is the amplifier; gate hard. MomentumDominance (A3) and RoleSplit (A4) refine it. FollowerFraction·Tilt ≈ 0.025 is the §4c starting imbalance.
Imbalance._Inertia_SentimentModulated_comment: Default-off: when true, a strong |shared global sentiment| shortens each bot's inertia hold (max→MinSec) so the fleet re-decides fast + together during shared-sentiment periods (for cross-stock correlation).
Imbalance._reactionPersistence_comment: §reaction-persistence split: decouples FAST reaction (bot re-decides every decision) from SLOW persistence (a per-bot AR(1) pressure decaying over minutes), coupled to a TAKER override so persistent conviction crosses the spread. Default off/neutral ⇒ byte-identical; when on it SUPERSEDES Inertia + the reaction/hold levers. ReactionTauSec is reserved (0 = inert). Bake = ReactionPersistence + TakerCoupling on, Inertia off.

Anchor._comment: §A5 fast-anchor slack: widen the INTRADAY overheat band veto (0..1) so excursions can happen, while the slow value-anchor probability tilt still bounds price on the multi-hour scale. 0 = today.

Activity._comment: §Pillar B — self-exciting activity field A = G·S·B that makes volume CLUSTER and breathe. Off => every factor === 1 (byte-identical). Baseline<1 centers calm below 1 so quiet is genuinely quiet. WMoveDown>WMoveUp is the leverage effect. RECALIBRATED 2026-07-13 (BAKED with the composition seam): the fills self-excite channel (WSelf) AND the sentiment channel (low Theta) both saturated the field at prod fill/sentiment levels (h_eq >> cap => liquid names pinned at SMax ~90% of the time). Fix that shipped: WSelf 0 (sever the broken self-excite; the rings + move/news/sentiment drivers carry the dynamics), WMove cut ~8x (sustained per-tick EWMA return was over-exciting), Theta 0.9 (sentiment excites only at the tail, not the median). 4h bake: pin 45->8/50, field breathes. SMax is the hard backstop. Gamma is how hard volume concentrates on hot names (PickStock exponent).

Composition._comment: §composition seam — activity → order COMPOSITION (the price-moving channel; the cadence seam is scaler-absorbed). act = clamp(gNorm^GExp · S, Floor, Cap), gNorm = baseline-removed G (median 1). TakerExp k: post-pick per-stock override — hot names upgrade limits→slippage takers w/ prob 1-act^-k, quiet names downgrade takers→limits w/ prob 1-act^k (exact m·act^k cut); MM exempt; direction untouched. DistExp*: limit tier band × act^-k — keep DistExpFar 0 (StopOffset far-wall clamp ignores this factor). Both default 0 = byte-identical. Soak arm: TakerExp 0.5 + WSelf 0 (the fill self-excite channel is saturated at current fill rates — h_eq ≫ cap pins liquid names at SMax; see pin= in the Activity log line).
Composition._size_comment: §composition SIZE coupling (PROTOTYPE, default-off). order notional × clamp(act^SizeExp, 1/SizeCap, SizeCap): hot names trade bigger, quiet smaller (median-1). The COUNT-immune volume lever — the load-scaler pins order COUNT and taker-share changes MIX, so per-order SIZE is what can move volume CV (0.17 shipped vs real ~1.5-2). Runaway-safe on the shipped config: WSelf 0 severs the fills->S->size Hawkes loop, RecordFill excites on fill-COUNT not notional, SizeCap bounds one order, downstream cash/room/depth clamps keep CK=0. MM exempt. SizeExp 0 = off, byte-identical.
Composition._openramp_comment: §open taker ramp (seam council 5/5): for OpenRampMin minutes after (re)start, taker orders convert to resting limits with prob 1−clamp((uptimeMin−stagger)/Ramp,0,1); per-stock hashed stagger in [0, OpenRampStaggerMin] de-synchronizes the 50 charts' re-valuation kickoff. Bleeds the fresh-fleet transient out gradually (a blackout stores it and releases it at once). DEFAULT 0 = off, byte-identical — flipped by docs/RESEED_RUNBOOK.md for reseeds only (soak baselines keep the raw open). Runbook values: OpenRampMin 10, OpenRampStaggerMin 8.

Mood._comment: §fear-greed composite index (0=fear,50=neutral,100=greed) — the FAST layer of the one-axis-three-timescales model (sentiment slow anchor → F&G composite → activity/taker expression). mood = 50 + 50·tanh(WMom·momZ + WBreadth·(2·breadth−1) − WVol·volZ + WFlow·flowZ + WSent·sentiment). Momentum leads (it's the liveliness driver, fixing v1's 'too smooth' sentiment×activity proxy); a vol SPIKE is fear; buy taker-flow + a broad rally are greed; sentiment is demoted to a small slow anchor (~0.25). momZ = EWMA(ret,MomTau) ÷ its EWMA σ; volZ = EWMA(|ret|,VolTau)/baseline − 1; flowZ = (buy−sell)/(buy+sell) taker notional. Enabled=false ⇒ the endpoint keeps the v1 fallback (byte-identical) so nothing regresses until the composite is soak-validated, then flipped on. Tau values in seconds (real-time sim).

Range._comment: §C1/C3 microstructure — wicks. ActivityImpact relaxes the per-order slippage cap on HOT names (scaled by S, needs Activity:Enabled) up to MaxSlippage so sweeps print wicks; the structural anti-sweep DEPTH cap (Liquidity:MaxSweepFractionOfDepth) stays the hard ceiling. FatImpactProb gives a minority of market orders a deeper sweep so spikes print in calm periods.

Bots._downdrift_comment: §bot-down-drift-fix (validated 2026-06-10, 2.5h soak): GreedStyle neutralizes the Panic 2:1 sell-skew; CashHomeostasis:Continuous (MaxShift 0.45) is a restoring force toward the cash-band midpoint (== seed cash% after the /Tools recenter) that bounds the residual down-drift within the <=5%/4h budget (medianAbs ~3%/2.5h, sub-linear) with movement intact (steady-state body/range == RW). Off = today's behaviour. Residual creep is the buy-passive/sell-aggressive taker asymmetry (a code-level follow-up, not a config knob).

CashHomeostasis._comment_stage5: R4 §0009 Stage 5A (final): MaxShift 0.45 → 0.30 + EdgeForce softened to 0.65/0.35. Stage 6 (Tools/ BUY_BIAS_BASE 0.45→0.35) shifted mean_homeo from +0.619 → +0.519 as predicted but anchor contribution rose to 90% to compensate — system has multiple buy-pressure components that fill in for each other. The 5pp symmetry gate needs an engine-level fix beyond parameter tuning; reserved for round 5. Stage 5A ships as the structurally-improved-but-bounded final state.

Bots._liquidityaware_comment: R4 §0009 Stage 4 — Option D: tilt limit-order offsets by book imbalance so adding to the thick side pushes the limit further from mid (less likely to thicken further) while adding to the thin side pulls it closer (helps fill the gap). Gain 0.30 means at fully one-sided book the offset shifts by 30%. 0 = off ⇒ byte-identical limit placement.
Bots._sentimentdynamics_comment: §bot-sentiment-dynamics: the slope-aware phase model. Enabled=false ⇒ byte-identical to today (no EWMA-slope compute; the old momentum + sentiment-bias + ApplyExtremeReaction path stays live). On: per-stock two-timescale EWMA slope ds drives a per-strategy DirectionalBias(s, ds, lateness L) that REPLACES the level-only directional terms AND ApplyExtremeReaction (no double-acting). Momentum strategies TAKE liquidity ∝ |bias| (symmetric, so no taker-flow down-drift). The value anchor + CashHomeostasis above remain the reversion floor. Tuning order (per §1b): calibrate SlopeScale* from the logged dsSlow max/mean, set reversion+anchor first, then raise MomentumConviction/ScalperConviction toward loop gain G≈1 (trends + reversals, drift ≤5%/4h). Run with Imbalance:Herding off to avoid double-driving.
Bots._sentiment_pricereaction_comment: Realism #2: contrarian price->sentiment feedback. A SUSTAINED move pushes sentiment the OTHER way so a long-lived slow-ring bias can't print a linear drift. Leaky-integrates the per-tick return over ReactTauSec (~ fractional move over the window, tick-rate-stable), ignores moves smaller than ReactDeadband, and adds a contrarian term clamped to +/-ReactCap. Slow tau + dead-band keep the 1-min scale (ret_acf_lag1) untouched. Off => byte-identical (and on leaves the OU rings' RNG untouched).

Sentiment._sigmamult_comment: §cross-stock CORRELATION lever: multipliers over the (hardcoded) sentiment-ring σ arrays. Cross-stock return correlation = shared²/(shared²+idiosyncratic²); today it's ~0.08 because the PER-STOCK rings swamp the shared GLOBAL ring. LOWER PerStockSigmaMult (<1) + RAISE GlobalSigmaMult (>1) ⇒ the shared common-mode dominates ⇒ higher correlation (markets move together). Compose with SlowRingDamp + per-stock personality amplitude. NOTE the idiosyncratic budget also includes RegimeDrift — reaching 0.2-0.5 likely needs RegimeDrift:Strength down too. 1.0/1.0 ⇒ byte-identical.
Sentiment._mom_comment: #3 waves: a FAST positive-feedback (momentum) term composing with the slow contrarian above — price up briefly lifts sentiment (FOMO chase) over MomTauSec, then the slow contrarian sours it => boom-bust waves. MomStrength=0 => #3 off even when PriceReaction is on.
Sentiment._slowringdamp_comment: Realism: multiplier on the SLOW per-stock OU rings (τ≥1000s ⇒ the 1800s/10800s sustained-bias scales) — lowers the SOURCE of linear price drift without touching the fast bounding scales. 1.0 = off (byte-identical).
Sentiment._regimedrift_comment: System A: per-stock PERSISTENT common-mode bounded random walk (NOT mean-reverting like the OU rings) so price TRENDS/wanders for minutes. step std = StepSigma·√dt; cubic soft-wall keeps it free in the middle but walled at ±Cap; Strength scales its push into combined sentiment. Enabled=false ⇒ byte-identical. Pair with AbsoluteCapMax + ValueAnchor as runaway backstops.
Sentiment._comovement_comment: Cross-stock CO-MOVEMENT: a SINGLE shared bounded-random-walk 'market factor' that every stock loads onto via a deterministic per-stock beta (~1.0 ± BetaSpread, hash of stockId), so the cohort co-moves (market-wide up/down days, sector swings). Sibling of RegimeDrift but ONE shared walk (RegimeDrift is per-stock INDEPENDENT ⇒ ~0 cross-stock corr). CHANNEL = the FUNDAMENTAL anchor target (not sentiment, which the value-anchor DAMPS): Strength×beta×factor is a fractional shift composed onto each stock's FundamentalService target at read time, so the value-anchor pulls all stocks together (a market-wide repricing the anchor SUPPORTS). factor walk bounded by Cap/SoftWallK; the composed anchor target is bounded to seed×[1±(Fundamental:Band + ShiftCap)]. Targets cross-stock return correlation 0.003→~0.2-0.4 (real ~0.2-0.5). Enabled=false ⇒ byte-identical; drift-bounded. RNG-disciplined (no reseed; beta from stockId hash).
Sentiment._globalshock_comment: §global-shock: a discrete, occasional, MARKET-WIDE bearish sentiment event = the 'elevator down' + cross-stock CORRELATION. ONE Poisson stream for the whole market (MeanIntervalHours); on fire a signed magnitude (min+span·U^exp) adds to a single scalar that decays per tick (DecayPerTick) and folds into EVERY stock's sentiment => all stocks drop together AND the whole fleet turns bearish at once (=> fleet-wide bear-short, the down-move a single aggressor can't make since the book absorbs one source). DownBias = P(event is bearish) (~0.85 = correlated fear, rare up melt-ups). Dedicated RNG => Enabled=false is byte-identical. Pair with Bots:BearShortStrength (the amplifier).

Bots._economylog_comment: Cadence (seconds) of the BotEconomy telemetry snapshot — an O(bots×positions) walk that is the largest residual periodic-maint chunk (~40-70ms) after the B3 lean-reload fix. 60 = current/byte-identical; raise it (e.g. 300) to sample the snapshot down and shrink maint further. Telemetry-only: zero CK / behaviour / realism effect (just logs the economy line less often).
Bots._stopmaxage_comment: §stop-ttl (INTERIM — see docs/ultraplan-prompt-maint-tick-scaling.md; the proper fix is B2, don't scan armed stops). Standalone armed stops have NO expiry (retention leaves them) so they pile up unbounded (~58/bot on prod) and bloat the O(book) maint prune scan (tick 250→450ms). StopMaxAgeSec>0 gives standalone armed stops (bracket children excluded) a per-order jittered TTL, cancelled via the SAFE per-order path (releases the arm reservation; watcher promote is defensive on !IsArmed). StopCullMaxPerSweep caps the per-sweep drain so a large backlog can't mass-cancel in one tick. 0 = off / byte-identical.
Bots._stopleak_comment: BAKED DEFAULT-ON (2026-07-09) — validated on prod (maint 296→8-70ms, tick 483→~200ms, CK=0, pool draining). Workstream 1 (docs/ultraplan-prompt-maint-tick-scaling.md): the cure for the armed-stop maint blowup. StopReplaceOld: before a bot arms a new standalone protective stop, cancel its prior (stock,side) standalone armed stop via the SAFE per-order path (bots MOVE a stop instead of STACKING one) — cures the additive source. PruneLimitOnly (B2): PruneWorstOrdersAsync iterates a per-bot limit-only mirror of OpenOrders so the ~30s scan is O(limits), independent of stop count. Needs StopMaxAgeSec=0 (interim retired). Rollback = false + restart.
Bots._leanreload_comment: BAKED DEFAULT-ON (2026-07-09) — the ACTUAL tick fix, validated on prod (maint 8-70ms INDEPENDENT of the 855k pool). Workstream 1b/B3: the ~60s reload (RefreshAssetsAsync) fetches only open LIMITS + a per-bot armed-stop COUNT (index-only via the IX_Orders_ArmedStop* partial indexes, auto-applied on startup) instead of the ~1.18M armed-stop rows => reload O(limits) not O(pool); the open-order cap adds the count, replace-old queries its victims. Requires the armed-stop partial-index migration. Rollback = false + restart.
Bots._sourcecap_comment: §source-cap (docs/ultraplan-prompt-maint-tick-scaling-md-pure-abelson.md) — the DISEASE CURE for the unbounded armed-stop pool. StopReplaceOld only nets within the same (stock,side), so a bot watching many stocks still accumulates one stop per (stock,side). MaxArmedStopsPerBot>0 REJECTS a bot's new protective-stop arm once it already holds that many armed stops (reject-at-placement — cancels nothing, so zero cancel/reservation/CK exposure), bounding the pool to ≈cap×fleet (DB + StopTriggerWatcher index). Requires LeanReload=true (the per-bot count lives in ctx.ArmedStopCount, kept exact by a +1 on arm / −1 on replace-old, re-baselined by the ~60s GROUP-BY) and StopMaxAgeSec=0 (the aged cull removes armed stops without decrementing). 0 = off/byte-identical. Soak-gated (CK=0, pool bounded, market realism unchanged). ArmedStopCapProbe logs blocked/armed/poolTotal each ~60s to confirm the gate fires.
Bots._anchortiming_comment: R5 §B+C anchor-timing fix for the structural −0.43 1-min return autocorrelation. B (AnchorReactionLag): each bot perceives a Lateness-lagged EWMA of the anchor tilt (alpha = MaxAlpha − L·(MaxAlpha−MinAlpha)) so the cohort's correction spreads across minutes instead of snapping back in lockstep. C (AnchorDeadbandPrc): anchors hold zero pull within ±band of the target (deviation units), only the excess corrects. All default-off ⇒ byte-identical.
Bots._orderwall_comment: Order-wall declumping: a fraction (RoundSnapProb) of limit orders snap toward psychologically-significant round levels. RoundSnapSpread=0 snaps to the exact level (builds a single wall); >0 disperses snapped orders within +/-spread*unit so volume forms a natural cluster across nearby ticks instead of one impassable wall. Defaults (0.30, 0) reproduce the prior exact-snap behavior byte-for-byte.
Bots._bounce_comment: Microstructure bid-ask bounce (Roll 1984): the last-trade close zig-zags across the spread each print, injecting mechanical negative 1-min ret_acf. (a) BounceReference=mid|micro records a bounce-free reference (mid=(bid+ask)/2, micro=size-weighted) sampled from the book ONCE per taker at trade time; the realism scorer's candle CLOSE and the app candle close key off it instead of last-trade. (b) PriceTickDecimals>0 rounds the printed/quoted price to a finer grid (extra decimals beyond the currency default) so each fill snaps less; cash/notional/reservation stay on the currency grid so conservation is unaffected. Defaults (off, 0) are byte-identical to last-trade. Stamped only as a reference series — Transaction.Price (the cash price) is unchanged.
Bots._fx_comment: FX mid-rate realism (FxRateService AR(1) walker: x_new = Alpha*x_old + (1-Alpha)*base + Amplitude*base*U(-1,+1), clamped to base*(1 +/- RateBand)). The market-structure council (2026-07-04) found the FX rate was the one genuinely-unrealistic piece (~50-100x too volatile, too arithmetic); the fix is a mean-reverting bounded walk at ~1% intraday vol. Alpha = mean-reversion persistence (higher = smoother, more random-walk-like, longer reversion half-life); Amplitude = per-tick (per-minute) noise size = the primary VOLATILITY dial; RateBand = the hard +/- clamp (cosmetic at low Amplitude since the walk sits ~10 std inside it, but tidies the absurd +/-20%); ConvertSpread = half the round-trip FX bid/ask = the USD/EUR arb coupling FLOOR AND the house's arb-conversion revenue source (leave for now; narrowing it starves the house unless decoupled to a fee). BAKED DEFAULT-ON (2026-07-09): the damped values Alpha 0.97 / Amplitude 0.002 / RateBand 0.05 (was the historical 0.92 / 0.005 / 0.20) — validated clean (per-min FX vol 0.30%->0.12%, intraday swing 3.5%->1.7% ~= 1% target, house/arb revenue intact via untouched ConvertSpread, CK=0). Rollback = restore 0.92 / 0.005 / 0.20 + restart. Read once at startup via FxRateService.Configure; no reseed.
Bots._directionallag_comment: #1: Lateness-staggered EWMA lag on the fast directional/sentiment (slope) reaction — the loop the bounce diagnostic isolated as the genuine ~-0.31 1-min mean-reversion driver (anchors were ruled out by R5 B-alone). alpha = MaxAlpha - L*(MaxAlpha-MinAlpha): early bots react fast, late bots slow, spreading the cohort's next-minute overcorrection. Default-off => byte-identical.
Bots._perceivedpricedesync_comment: §perceived-price desync (the 72% fleet reaction-loop lever): the cleaner 'what price does each bot even SEE' version of DirectionalReactionLag. When on, each bot reacts to its OWN fast+slow perceived-price EWMA instead of the SHARED sentiment slope every other bot reads each tick — the EWMA rate is dispersed per bot by BOTH Lateness AND a salted hash of AiUserId, so even same-Lateness, same-tick bots perceive different prices and the cohort stops snapping its own 1-min print back in lockstep (the ret_acf_lag1 driver). The per-bot slope = (live - perceived)/perceived feeds DirectionalBias via its OWN Tanh scales (PerceivedSlopeScaleFast/Slow) because the perceived-return gap is a different unit from the sentiment slope — these scales are the PRIMARY A/B dial. MinAlpha/MaxAlpha are the dispersion band (L=0 fastest~MaxAlpha, L=1 slowest~MinAlpha). SUPERSEDES DirectionalReactionLag (its output tilt-lag is skipped when this is on) and is NOT meant to be co-enabled with it or the ImpactDecouple* flags. Pure/RNG-free (no Random draw, no wall-clock). Default-off => byte-identical; rollback = set false + restart.
Bots._refillthrottle_comment: §refill-throttle (the bot-behavior REFILL RESPONSE lever): the realism ceiling is the fast-refilling order book — ~20k bots re-post resting limits at the OLD level every ~1s tick, so a taker that eats the resisting wall sees it instantly rebuilt and the mid never moves (jump probe: a 4-7% jump realizes ~0.1%). On a CONFIRMED mover this gate makes refill RESPOND: (OffsetWiden.Mult) widens the RESISTING side's limit offset, and/or (SkipRepost.Prob) skips re-posting the resisting-side limit, so the wall the move pushes into stops reforming and the next unit of pressure shifts the mid. It is a BOUNDED control loop (NOT open-loop — that was the BuyStopFraction failure): the SIGNAL is fill-derived realized return (Signal.Source=RealizedReturnFast — non-circular, self-extinguishing), a Schmitt trigger arms at ThresholdArm and disarms at ThresholdDisarm with a RearmCooldownTicks, and a per-event move-budget cap (Control.MaxEventMovePct, the 4-7% band) force-disarms so the move arrests instead of running away. Bot-decision layer ONLY (no matching/settlement change) ⇒ CK-safe; perf-neutral-to-positive (fewer resting orders on movers). Default Enabled=false ⇒ the context holds a NULL gate, every call site is byte-identical and consumes no RNG; the single skip-repost seeded draw is taken only when enabled AND the order resists. Probe=true logs widen/skip engagement (first-minute 0 ⇒ inert-flag trap). DEPENDS on a non-zero ThresholdArm to engage. Rollback = Enabled=false + restart.
Bots._impactdecouple_comment: §impact-decouple (ret_acf_lag1 ceiling): HARD structural break of the 1-min self-impact reaction loop (the cohort pushes price then fades its OWN 1-min move next minute). A (ImpactDecoupleReference): the directional reaction measures price against a >1-min EWMA reference (ReferenceHalfLifeSec, range 150-300) instead of the ~1s prior price, so it responds to multi-minute trend not the self-made wiggle. The live channel under SentimentDynamics is the value/recent ANCHOR GAP; the legacy momentum / personal-sentiment / sentiment price-reaction channels are also rerouted for non-dynamics modes. The slow anchor TARGETS (fundamental, recent EWMA) are unchanged, so the multi-minute restoring force / runaway guard survives. B (ImpactDecoupleHold): a per-bot >1-min refractory (HoldWindowSec, range 75-150, FLOOR >60s = the scorer bucket) holds the combined directional stance so a bot cannot reverse within the minute it acted - the HARD version of DirectionalReactionLag, acting on the SAME directional term and NOT meant to be co-enabled with it. ImpactHoldProbe logs B's held-fraction for soak liveliness (default off). All default-off => byte-identical; rollback = set the two bools false and restart.
Bots._exogshock_comment: §exogenous-information (ret_acf_lag1 ceiling): a per-stock stream of signed, decaying news shocks (RandomShockSource: rate-based Poisson arrival 1-exp(-dt/MeanIntervalMinutes), power-law magnitude in [Min,Max] fraction of seed, accumulator soft-walled + hard-clamped to +/-Cap). AnchorTracksShock makes the value-anchor TARGET = current*(1+shock) (FundamentalService.Get), bounded to seed*[1 +/- (Band+Cap)] - it is REFUSED at startup unless Enabled && ValueAnchor:CapFromSeed && ValueAnchor:AbsoluteCapMax>0 && Fundamental:Band+Cap < AbsoluteCapMax (keeps the moving target provably interior to the hard veto = no runaway). The chaser cohort (salted, per-(bot,shock) hash-selected slice of eligible bots) submits a real marketable (slippage-market) order INTO the live shock on a chase tick, REPLACING that bot's normal order - direct persistent 1-min directional VOLUME (the actual VWAP ret_acf lever; the retired ChaserStrength*tanh buyProb tilt only shifted the buy/sell ratio and could not move it). ChaserNotionalFrac is the primary dial (0 => off, byte-identical); ChaserMaxNotionalFrac caps each order as a fraction of the bot's SEED-price portfolio value (mark-independent, so chasing a rising price cannot inflate its own order size); ChaserFraction is a coarse cohort-size cap (<=0.25). Chase orders ride the same OrderEntry->Match->Settle path (no naked flow) and inherit the depth-cap + value-band veto. ChaserStrength/ChaserScale are RETIRED (kept only for back-compat logging, feed nothing). GlobalFraction (0=off) makes a fraction of shock arrivals fire MARKET-WIDE - one shared signed magnitude applied to EVERY stock the same tick (dedicated RNG, byte-identical at 0) - so the anchor moves and the chaser cohort trades INTO the shock on ALL stocks at once = shared TAKER FLOW = cross-stock correlation (the sentiment-tilt channel is damped per-stock; shared FLOW is not). Pair with Enabled=true + ChaserNotionalFrac>0 for real correlated volume. Run the sweep with AnchorTracksShock=false so a genuine mean-reversion restoring force survives (else a 'win' may just be disabled negative feedback) and the band veto binds against a seed-pinned fundamental. Calibrate MeanIntervalMinutes+DecayHalfLifeSec jointly to a live-shock duty cycle >=~60%. All default-off/inert => GetShock==0, Tick no-op, no chase tilt => byte-identical; rollback = Enabled=false + restart.

ExogShock._globalcofire_comment: §global co-fire (cross-stock correlation via SHARED taker flow): on the tick a MARKET-WIDE pulse fires (needs Enabled + GlobalFraction>0), a GlobalCoFireFraction cohort submits ONE same-sign marketable order on a hash-SPREAD watchlist stock THIS tick - bypassing the chaser's cadence + argmax-concentration - at GlobalCoFireNotionalFrac*seedPortfolio. = a simultaneous, correlated taker BURST across all stocks (the plan-review fix for the prior null, where the capped + time-spread + argmax-concentrated chaser was book-absorbed: co-fire fixes all three - same-TICK, same-SIGN, SPREAD). Draw-free (0 RNG). WATCH PERF: a big cohort = a market-order burst on the pulse tick on a commit-bound loop (cap must hold >=~19k during shocks or the corr number is invalid). Default GlobalCoFire=false => byte-identical; rollback = false + restart.
ExogShock._sector_comment: §sector pulse (intra-sector correlation): a SectorFraction of MARKET-WIDE pulses instead scope to ONE sector (sector = stockId % SectorCount) - the co-fire cohort pushes only that sector => intra-sector-high / cross-sector-low correlation (the real-market signature + sector rotation for free). SectorCount 1 OR SectorFraction 0 => every pulse market-wide => byte-identical. Lightweight stockId%N map (no reseed); a real Stock.Sector column + UI = the follow-up.

Bots._jumps_comment: §fat-tail jumps (return-kurtosis lever): a RARE per-stock Poisson price JUMP (RandomJumpSource: dedicated seed 97, rate-based arrival 1-exp(-dt/(MeanIntervalHours*3600)), power-law target magnitude in [MinPct,MaxPct], per-event RANDOM sign). On fire JumpService walks the book with a short burst of REAL marketable (slippage-market) orders from a dedicated house aggressor (AggressorUserId, default 20100 - seeded with cash + a per-stock share float by Tools/GenerateAIUsers.py, NOT the FX-desk house 20002 which holds zero shares), self-bounded to the target % within ONE 1-min bucket (stop at target / book-dry / MaxSlices), so it momentarily EXCEEDS the per-tick band+depth caps (those live in the decision layer, which this bypasses) WITHOUT exhausting the book. It then sustains AftershockBuckets buckets of small decaying alternating-sign nudges (~1 candle apart) so volatility clustering (absret_acf_lag1) is preserved, and does NOT move the fundamental anchor (distinct from ExogShock:AnchorTracksShock) - so ValueAnchor + AbsoluteCapMax pull the price back SLOWLY over minutes (mean-reversion => a tail event, not a level shift; no fast counter-order or ret_acf blows out). DriftGuardPct flips an event's sign if price already left the band that way. All orders ride the same OrderEntry->Match->Settle path (CK=0; ConservationProbe/ReservationAuditor cover them). Calibrate MeanIntervalHours so E[events/stock]~1 over the soak (kurtosis estimator has a median/mean cliff below ~0.7) and MinPct/MaxPct to ~6-8x per-stock sigma. Default Enabled=false => no RNG drawn, Tick no-op => byte-identical; rollback = Enabled=false + restart. Set Probe=true for the JUMP liveliness log (fired/meanPct/net/gross).

Jumps._aggressorIdComment: MUST equal NUM_PEOPLE + Config.py JUMP_AGGRESSOR_USER_ID_OFFSET. NUM_PEOPLE=19783 (fleet+arb5+MM12+rotator200=20,000 total, Kiesh 20k cap); chain house(2)+admin(1)+arb(5)+MM(12)+rotator(200)=220 ⇒ 19783+220=20003. Update together with any cohort-size/NUM_PEOPLE change and regenerate the xlsx.

Bots._staggering_comment: Bot staggering: phase-offset each bot's act cadence across ticks so only ~1/Slots of the cohort decides+places per tick (Slots = the per-tick load-cut factor N). A bot in slot (AiUserId % Slots) acts only on ticks where (TickId % Slots) matches its slot — a pure deterministic function of (bot id, tick), NO RNG, so seed reproducibility holds. Cuts the per-COMMIT DB round-trip load per tick, letting the scaler run a higher equilibrium bot count at the same setpoint. Default-off (Enabled=false) => byte-identical.
Bots._scaler_comment: §B scaler control-loop levers. All default-off => loadFrac == ewma/interval, byte-identical (the scaler still moves ActiveBotCap exactly as before). DutyCycleDenominator: divide tick-work by the TRUE period (interval + ewma) instead of the bare interval — the loop's fixed Task.Delay(TradeInterval) makes the real period 1000+ewma, so ewma/1000 overstates load and holds the cap well below capacity; enabling it cranks the cap UP, so the tick-<=-interval guard (TickGuardFraction, code-default 1.0 = inert) AUTO-LOWERS to 0.95 when this is on, refusing a cap increase once the full tick fills 95% of the period. Override TickGuardFraction here (0.5..1.0) to tune the guard. ActionableSpanSizing: size the cap from the Collect+Batch span the scaler can act on, excluding the cap-exempt cohorts (arb/mm/rotator/jump/drain). MaxTickMultiple: R2-1 operator-facing tick-cadence ceiling for the corrected path — engages ONLY with DutyCycleDenominator on (ignored/byte-identical when off). It names a tick-WORK multiple k (work ewma <= k*interval) and recenters the WHOLE control band on k (guard=target=k/(1+k), shrink at k*1.25, grow below k*0.80), so the cap settles at ewma ~= k*interval instead of racing to MaxBotCap; this supersedes the inert 0.95 auto-guard and the fixed 0.60 target. PRODUCT CURVE (surface for Kiesh, don't decide): k=1 => tick ~= interval / moderate cap (soak reports the value vs control's ~1575); k=2..4 => coarser 2-4s tick for up to ~7x throughput / all-20k active. Sweep k in a soak and read the trade curve before any flip. SelfCorrectingDelay: subtract elapsed work from the end-of-tick delay so the period tracks TradeInterval — NOT byte-identical (changes which bots are due each tick), ships last, own flag. Every ON path is behavior-changing (moves the cap trajectory) => re-tune + soak to the commit-bound equilibrium; the human owns the flip.

Platform._comment: §3.7 reserved platform house account — a pure PROFIT account. The bulk FROM↔TO swap settles against the external FX market at mid (currency enters/leaves the sim there; total value at mid stays conserved), so the desk holds no inventory and never depletes — only the conversion spread accrues here. Seeded (Identity + Holding, no AIUser Profile) as UserId 20002 by Tools/GenerateAIUsers.py; the seed is now vestigial (the desk no longer needs starting inventory). Excluded from the random fleet, cash injection, and retention prune.

Candles._comment: §filtered-tape H/L (the SIP odd-lot / TradingView rule): fills below HLMinFillSize count toward volume/close/vwap but do NOT set candle High/Low — tiny prints sweeping thin levels are non-representative of the accessible market and real consolidated tapes exclude them from the official range. 0 = off (byte-identical). Council-validated value: 10 (≈ median fill; counterfactual on the 4h ref tape: body/range p50 0.42→0.47 = real MSFT 1-min, extreme-wick candles −40%). RE-MEASURE after any reseed (fill-size distribution shifts; see reseed checklist). The CLIENT builds the live in-progress bar via the shared CandleAggregator — set the same value in the client Resources/Raw/appsettings.json when flipping.
